performance_criteria.description: Wear clean, smart and appropriate personal Protective Equipment.
Work safely at all times, complying with health and safety and other relevant regulations and Guidelines.
Identify appropriate person to treat any cuts, grazes and wounds.
Report illness and infection promptly to the appropriate Persons.
State own responsibility under the health and safety Act as it relates to own occupation.
State general rules on hygiene that must be followed.
State correct Personal Protection Equipment such as Head Protection, Foot Protection, Face and eye Protection, Hand and Body protection and regulatory protection.
State the importance of maintaining good personal Hygiene.
Describe how to deal with cuts, grazes and wounds and why it is important to do so

learning_outcomes.description: Maintain a hygienic, safe and hazard free workplace.

performance_criteria.description: State the importance of working in a healthy, safe and hygienic workplace
Report any accidents or near accidents quickly and accurately to the proper person.
Follow health, hygiene and safety procedures during work
Practice emergency procedures during work
Follow organizational security procedures
Ensure the disposal of waste and pollution control with organic and Inorganic waste disposal methods.
Follow sound and noise control and protection methods
Identify any hazards or potential hazards and deal with these correctly.
State where information about health and safety in your workplace can be obtained
Describe the types of hazards in the workplace that may occur and how to deal with them
State hazards that can be dealt with personally and those that should be reported to someone else

learning_outcomes.description: Maintain a hygienic, safe and secure workplace

performance_criteria.description: State how to warn other people about hazards and why this is important
State why accidents and near accidents should be reported and who they should be reported to
Describe the types of emergencies that may happen in the workplace and how to deal with them
State where to find the first-aid equipment and who the registered first-aider is in the work place
State safe lifting and handling techniques that should be followed
State other ways of working safely that are relevant to own position and why they are important
Describe organizational emergencies procedures, in particular fire, and how these should be followed
State the possible causes for fire in the workplace
Describe how to minimize the possibility of fire in the workplace
State where to find the alarms and how to set them off
State why a fire should never be approached unless it is safe to do so.
State the importance of following the fire safety laws
Describe organizational security procedures and why these are Important
State the importance of reporting all usual or non- routine incidents to the appropriate personnel

units.title: Communication System in a Work Environment

learning_outcomes.description: Use simple communication system in a work environment

performance_criteria.description: Use a simple verbal means to pass on necessary information
Use nonverbal means to pass on necessary information e.g. body language
Identify symbols and signs appropriately

learning_outcomes.description: Develop the ability to identify the source of information in a work Environment.

performance_criteria.description: Locate the source of information in an organization and work Environment.
Relate appropriately with source of information
Use the general information flow systems in a work environment
Use information to avoid challenges in a work situation
Report findings in accordance to Procedure in a work environment.

learning_outcomes.description: Demonstrate the use of basic communicat- ion means in a work Environment...

performance_criteria.description: Locate the various communication equipment in the work environment
Use effectively the general communication equipment in a work Environment.
Pass information effectively to the right personnel
Pass information effectively using Symbols, signs and codes.
Comply with general instructions in line with ethics of the work Environment.

units.title: Team work in a work environment

performance_criteria.description: Identify the need for developing positive working relationship with Colleagues.
Recognize the importance of relating with other people in a way that makes them feel valued and Respected.
Assist team members when Required.
Report to the personnel when request for assistance fall outside area of responsibility.
Communicate information to colleagues about own work that might affect others.
Recognize own role and Responsibilities within the team.
Perform individual tasks in line with the team rules and Regulations.
Participate effectively in team Work.
Work in line with organizational Standard.
Use organization code of practice.
Explain organizational code of Conduct.

units.title: Identification of equipment, material handling and storage

performance_criteria.description: Identify manual and mechanically hand tools and equipment relevant to his/her Trade.
Select and use of hand tools and Equipment relevant to his/her trade.
Identify relevant materials to his/her trade.
Select and use of appropriate materials for a particular work.
Comply with the given information to Move and handle tools and equipment.
Select the required quality and quantity of resources for the method of moving and Handling tools and equipment.
Recognize individual work and team work for lifting, loading and unloading materials and equipment.
Follow sequence of loading and unloading of materials
Comply with the given information to store Resources.
Select the required quality and quantity of the resources for the method of storing resources.
Follow sequence of storing Resources.
Identify tools and Equipment required for Site clearance.
Carry out Site clearance
Assist in erection of temporary facilities
Identify basic tools used in excavation
Assist in setting out of the structure
Assist in carrying out excavation
Explain how to prepare shuttering against faces of excavation
Assist in erection of shuttering against faces of eccavation
Explain how and why surfaces of earth Should be treated.
Assist in treatment of earth
surfaces.
Describe the procedures involved in filling leveling and compaction of earth manually and mechanically.
Backfill excavated material
Compact earth manually
Assist to compact earth mechanically
Make the surface hard and clean from dirt/debris/grease or any other impurities
Identify and select tools, materials and equipment required for the Job
Describe the procedure of chiseling/ scraping and wet surface to Receive POP/Plaster.
Identify materials for its appropriate application
Describe the procedure of measuring materials by using appropriate measuring Devices.
Mix materials uniformly within the specification and stipulated time
Differentiate types of mortar and concrete mix

learning_outcomes.description: Differentiate uses and types of brick/block bonding

performance_criteria.description: Identify different uses and types of bonds
Describe different types of bonding
Participate in brick/block bonding works
Practice shifting and stacking arrangement of the required Materials for the job.
Lay blocks and bricks to line.
Describe how to produce dry bonding
Apply basic plastering and POP materials
Identify types of curing Methods and its timing.
Identify different types of scaffolding in the workplace.
Explain the different types of plat form/ shuttering in the workplace

learning_outcomes.description: Differentiate types of materials used in Scaffolding

performance_criteria.description: Identify types of materials used for Scaffolds
Describe types of materials being used for scaffolding on site
Identify and convey different types of scaffolding being used on site
Explain the method of erecting different types of scaffolding
Explain the method used in dismantling different types of scaffolding

learning_outcomes.description: Storage of Scaffolding materials Total learning hours 750 + Guided learning hours 300 Level Purpose: This qualification is designed to enable the learner demonstrate basic health and safety practices, and carry out construction activities using appropriate materials, tools and equipment and assisting the Senior Mason. supervise Assistant Mason and assist the Senior Mason in carrying out masonry work. U NIT 1 2 4 6

performance_criteria.description: Describe appropriate methods of stacking scaffolding materials before and after usage.
